info.title: Topos OSS plane
info.description: The self-hostable Topos plane — device-signed writes + token-scoped reads. Every returned protocol outcome rides in a 200 body (the canonical JsonEnvelope + receipt); non-2xx is reserved for transport/auth/integrity faults.

parameters.name: read_token
parameters.description: The per-follower read token (resolves to an opaque scope).

parameters.name: Topos-Known-Version-Id
parameters.description: The client's known current commit id (hex64) — the commit-sensitive half of the 304.

200.description: The signed current record (application/json) + a commit-sensitive ETag.

304.description: Pointer unchanged (the ETag AND the known version both match).

404.description: No such token, or no current pointer yet.

200.description: The proposal receipt (NEEDS_REVIEW on success; CONFLICT / DENIED / … otherwise).

400.description: Malformed body, identifier, or device signature.

200.description: The publish receipt — EVERY protocol outcome (OK / CONFLICT / APPROVAL_REQUIRED / DENIED / …).

400.description: Malformed body, identifier, or device signature.

200.description: The revert receipt (OK on success; CONFLICT / DENIED / … otherwise).

400.description: Malformed body, identifier, or device signature.

200.description: The review receipt (OK on an approve that promoted; CONFLICT / DENIED / … otherwise).

400.description: Malformed body, identifier, or device signature.

parameters.name: skill
parameters.description: Skill id (must match the read token's scope).

parameters.name: object_id
parameters.description: The content id (hex64) of the object to fetch.

404.description: No/blank credential, scope/path mismatch, or unreachable object.

parameters.name: skill
parameters.description: Skill id (must match the read token's scope).

parameters.name: version_id
parameters.description: The version (commit) id (hex64) whose metadata to read.

404.description: No/blank credential, scope/path mismatch, or unauthorized/unreachable version.

ActionCode.title: ActionCode
ActionCode.description: A machine-actionable next-action code. The values in `examples` are the known set; an unrecognized future code is still valid and MUST be executed via the action's `argv` (never rejected).

Affected.description: What an outcome refers to (every field optional — a policy flip has no skill/version).

version.description: A `version_id` (commit SHA-256, lowercase hex) when the outcome refers to a version.

version_id.description:
The full commit SHA-256 (lowercase hex) — the `version_id`. NOT the `bundle_digest`: the
pointer names the version, and the commit transitively pins the bytes.

Generation.description:
The internal anti-replay counter `(epoch, seq)`. NEVER rendered to a user; the
agent consumes `expected`/`current` on a CONFLICT.

JsonEnvelope.description:
The one common envelope every verb emits. Never prompts; prose (TTY) is rendered from the
SAME typed value where practical.

next_actions.description: Stable, machine-actionable next steps — each carries a complete argv.

oneOf.description: The durable idempotency record — present on **every** terminal op, including failures.

schema_version.description: Always `1` for this contract version (the schema pins it `const`).

NextAction.description:
A machine-actionable next step. The `argv` is the ready-to-exec command; `code` lets an agent
branch on the known set and still pass through unknowns.

argv.description: A complete argv array — execute as-is (no TTY parsing).

ProposeData.description:
`publish --propose` (opens a PR; uploads a full candidate **without moving `current`**). Returns
`NEEDS_REVIEW`. **INFERRED.**

ProposeRequest.description:
`POST /v1/proposals` body — opens a proposal (a PR): ingests a full candidate **without moving
`current` or signing** (`NEEDS_REVIEW`). The authority's `propose` op takes the **same** input shape as
`publish` (candidate + device + `op_id` + `expected`); there is **no** separate title/body on the op (a
title/body, if ever surfaced, would be composed into the candidate's commit message).

candidate.description: The full candidate bundle to ingest as the proposal's content.

device_key_id.description: The id of the device key whose signature (in the header) authorizes this op.

expected.description: The `(epoch, seq)` the proposal is born against (its base); a stale base later makes it non-current.

op_id.description: The client-minted UUIDv4 idempotency key (replays the stored receipt on retry).

skill_id.description: The target skill id within the workspace.

PublishData.description:
`publish` (a direct publish that moves `current`). On the FIRST publish the `/i/` link is
returned. Under `review-required` a direct publish instead returns `APPROVAL_REQUIRED` (with the
`publish --propose` next-action) and carries no `data`. **INFERRED.**

current_generation.description: The pointer's new generation after the move.

invite_link.description: Returned only on the first publish (which stands up the workspace): `/i/<token>`.

PublishRequest.description:
`POST /v1/publish` body — a direct publish that moves `current`. The device signature is the
`Topos-Device-Signature` header (not a body field); the server stamps `created_at`. Under
`review-required` the authority refuses this closed with `APPROVAL_REQUIRED`, ingesting nothing.

candidate.description: The full candidate bundle to ingest + publish.

device_key_id.description: The id of the device key whose signature (in the header) authorizes this op.

expected.description: The `(epoch, seq)` this publish's compare-and-set targets; a stale pair yields `CONFLICT`.

op_id.description: The client-minted UUIDv4 idempotency key — the same `op_id` replays the stored receipt byte-for-byte.

skill_id.description: The target skill id within the workspace.

workspace_id.description: The target workspace id (the receipt + pointer scope).

Receipt.description:
The ONE canonical receipt across all 11 outcomes — the durable idempotency record (present even
on failures): one stable receipt per op, identical on retry.

created_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp (the plane stamps it; never an ambient clock in `topos-core`).

key_id.description: The signing key id that covered this op.

op_id.description: Client-minted UUIDv4, persisted before the first send.

schema_version.description: Always `1` for this contract version (the schema pins it `const`).

RevertData.description:
`revert` (a **forward** git-revert restoring older bytes as a new, higher-`seq` version — never a
pointer rollback, never a delete). `--to` names the GOOD version. **INFERRED.**

new_version_id.description: The new forward-revert commit that carries those bytes.

reverted_to.description: The good version named by `--to` (the bytes being restored).

RevertRequest.description:
`POST /v1/reverts` body — a **forward** revert: the server constructs a new 1-parent commit carrying the
`good` version's bytes on top of `current` (`seq` advances; the pointer never moves backward). There is
**no candidate** — the server reads `good`'s tree + digest from its provenance and builds the commit.

author.description: The author device id recorded in the forward-revert commit frame.

device_key_id.description: The id of the device key whose signature (in the header) authorizes this op.

expected.description: The `(epoch, seq)` this revert's compare-and-set targets; a stale pair yields `CONFLICT`.

good.description: The GOOD version (the `version_id` whose bytes are restored) as 64-char lowercase hex.

op_id.description: The client-minted UUIDv4 idempotency key (replays the stored receipt on retry).

skill_id.description: The target skill id within the workspace.

ReviewData.description:
`review` (`--approve` / `--reject` a proposal). Approve is a compare-and-set on the base; a stale
base returns `CONFLICT`. **INFERRED.**

oneOf.description: The pointer's new generation when an approval moved `current`.

ReviewRequest.description:
`POST /v1/reviews` body — a governance decision on an open proposal. `approve` runs the shared
`(epoch, seq)` compare-and-set on the proposal's base (a stale base ⇒ `CONFLICT`) and, under
`review_required`, enforces four-eyes (the proposer may not self-approve) before promoting; `reject`
is a standalone status flip (nothing signed).

device_key_id.description: The id of the device key whose signature (in the header) authorizes this op.

expected.description:
The `(epoch, seq)` the approval's compare-and-set targets (the proposal's base); a stale pair on an
`approve` yields `CONFLICT`.

op_id.description: The client-minted UUIDv4 idempotency key (replays the stored receipt on retry).

proposal.description: The proposal being reviewed, named by its candidate commit id (64-char lowercase hex).

skill_id.description: The target skill id within the workspace.

value.description: base64url (unpadded) of the raw 64-byte Ed25519 signature — exactly 86 chars.

SignatureAlg.description:
The signature algorithm — a CLOSED set. v0 is Ed25519 only; an unknown or `none` algorithm must
fail to deserialize (fail closed), foreclosing algorithm-confusion / downgrade on the trust-root
pointer. (Contrast [`ActionCode`], deliberately OPEN for forward-compat — an algorithm id is the
exact opposite of a next-action code and must never silently pass through.)

SignedCurrentRecord.description: The signed `current` pointer — a versioned envelope; `ETag = "<epoch>.<seq>"`.

schema_version.description: Always `1` for this contract version (the schema pins it `const`).

TerminalOutcome.description: The closed set of terminal outcomes the agent branches on. SCREAMING_SNAKE on the wire.

WireCandidate.description:
A full candidate bundle: every file's bytes, the declared parents, and the author + message — the
shared input the `publish` and `propose` writes ingest (the `revert` write needs no candidate; the
server builds that forward commit from the good version). Maps to `plane-store`'s
`CandidateUpload { files, parents, author, message }` — each `parents` entry hex-decoded into a
`CommitId`.

author.description: The author device id recorded in the commit frame.

files.description: Every file in the candidate bundle (each server-rehashed).

message.description: The commit message (title + body composed into one string).

parents.description:
The candidate commit's declared parents, each a 64-char lowercase-hex `version_id` (`0` parents for a
genesis publish, `1` for a normal publish / propose, `2` for an author merge). Each must already be
present in the workspace; a lie changes the recomputed commit id, so the server need not trust it.

WireError.description:
The flat wire error (rich `thiserror` enums stay internal). Carries a
stable code + retryability + the safe next actions; never raw SQL/git strings.

code.description:
A stable, machine-branchable error code, distinct from (and finer than) `outcome`. This is an
**open, additive** string vocabulary — it is intentionally NOT a closed enum: new codes are
added over time, and a consumer must treat an unrecognized `code` as the `outcome` it carries.

context.description: Structured context (no TTY prose to parse).

WireFile.description:
One file of a candidate bundle, uploaded **by value**. The raw bytes ride as base64 in the JSON body
(`content_base64`, standard alphabet); the server base64-decodes them and **rehashes every byte** to
derive the content id — there is no reference-by-id, and a client hash is never trusted. Maps to
`plane-store`'s `UploadedFile { path, mode, bytes }` (decode `content_base64` → `bytes`).

content_base64.description: The raw file bytes, base64-encoded. The server decodes then rehashes them (server-side digest).

WireFileMode.description:
A candidate file's mode on the wire — the two git regular-file modes as their literal octal strings
(`"100644"` / `"100755"`). A **closed** wire mirror of `topos_core::digest::FileMode`: that kernel enum
lives in a `no_std` crate `topos-types` does not depend on (and it carries no serde/schema derives), so
the wire leaf owns its own copy. The route handler maps it 1:1 at the edge —
`Regular ⇔ FileMode::Regular`, `Executable ⇔ FileMode::Executable` — for both the inbound candidate
([`WireFile`]) and the outbound version metadata ([`WireVersionFile`]).

WireVersionFile.description:
One file of a version's metadata on the wire — its path, mode, and content id (`object_id`), mirroring
`plane-store`'s `VersionFile` with the id hex-encoded. The **bytes are NOT here**: a client fetches each
by `object_id` through the bundle (object) read route.

object_id.description: The file's content id (64-char lowercase hex) — the handle the per-blob read route resolves.

WireVersionMeta.description:
`GET /v1/workspaces/{ws}/skills/{skill}/versions/{version_id}` response body — a version's authenticated
metadata: its id, the COMPLETE parent set, display author + message, the consent `bundle_digest`, and the
per-file `(path, mode, object_id)` leaves. Mirrors `plane-store`'s `VersionMeta` with every 32-byte id
hex-encoded. Assembled WITHOUT reading any blob bytes; the `bundle_digest` is the pin the client's
per-blob fetches + its own re-hash must reproduce.

author.description: The author device id from the commit frame.

bundle_digest.description: The byte-exact consent hash over the bundle (64-char lowercase hex) — the fetch + re-hash pin.

files.description: The per-file leaves, in the version's recorded order.

message.description: The commit message (title + body as one string).

parents.description:
The COMPLETE parent set, each a 64-char lowercase-hex commit id (`0` for genesis, `1` normally, `2`
for an author merge).

version_id.description: This version's commit id (64-char lowercase hex).
